name: $Context
summary: $Context is a string giving the current context. New symbols are created

name: $ContextPath
summary: $ContextPath is a list of contexts used (in order) to resolve bare

name: Abs
summary: Abs[z] gives the absolute value (modulus) of numeric z, Sqrt[Re[z]^2 + Im[z]^2] for complex z.

name: Apart
summary: Apart[expr] rewrites a rational expression as a sum of terms with minimal denominators.

name: Append
summary: Append[expr, elem] adds elem to the end of expr.

name: Arg
summary: Arg[z] gives the argument (phase angle in (-Pi, Pi]) of numeric z; 0 for nonnegative reals, Pi for negative reals.

name: Attributes
summary: Attributes[s] gives the list of attributes for s.

name: Begin
summary: Begin["ctx`"] sets the current context ($Context) to "ctx`", saving

name: BeginPackage
summary: BeginPackage["ctx`"] sets the current context to "ctx`" and restricts

name: Blank
summary: _ or Blank[] represents any single expression.

name: BlankNullSequence
summary: ___ or BlankNullSequence[] represents a sequence of zero or more expressions.

name: BlankSequence
summary: __ or BlankSequence[] represents a sequence of one or more expressions.

name: Block
summary: Block[{x, y, ...}, expr] evaluates expr with local values for x, y, ....

name: ByteCount
summary: ByteCount[expr] gives the number of bytes used internally by Mathilda to store expr.

name: Cancel
summary: Cancel[expr] cancels out common factors in the numerator and denominator of expr.

name: Cases
summary: Cases[{e1, e2, ...}, pattern] gives a list of the ei that match the pattern.

name: ClearAttributes
summary: ClearAttributes[s, attr] removes attr from the list of attributes of s.

name: CoefficientList
summary: CoefficientList[poly, var] gives a list of coefficients of powers of var in poly, starting with power 0.

name: Commonest
summary: Commonest[list] gives a list of the elements that are the most common in list.

name: CompoundExpression
summary: expr1; expr2; ... evaluates its arguments in sequence, returning the last result.

name: Conjugate
summary: Conjugate[z] gives the complex conjugate Re[z] - I Im[z] of numeric z; real and real-valued (Re/Im/Abs/Arg) arguments are returned unchanged.

name: Count
summary: Count[list, pattern] gives the number of elements in list that match pattern.

name: Cubics
summary: Cubics is an option for Solve that controls whether cubic

name: D
summary: D[f, x] gives the partial derivative of f with respect to x.

name: Delete
summary: Delete[expr, n] deletes the element at position n in expr.

name: DeleteCases
summary: DeleteCases[expr, pattern] removes all elements of expr that match pattern.

name: Derivative
summary: f' represents the derivative of a function f of one argument.

name: DesignMatrix
summary: DesignMatrix[data, {f1, ..., fn}, vars] gives the design matrix with entries f_i evaluated at the data coordinates.

name: DiagonalMatrix
summary: DiagonalMatrix[list] gives a matrix with the elements of list on the leading diagonal, and zero elsewhere.

name: DifferenceDelta
summary: DifferenceDelta[f, i] gives the forward difference (f /. i -> i+1) - f, the discrete analogue of D. It is the left inverse of indefinite Sum.

name: DigitCount
summary: DigitCount[n] gives a list of the counts of digits 1, 2, ..., 9, 0 in the base-10 representation of n.

name: Divide
summary: x / y or Divide[x, y] represents x / y; rewritten by the evaluator to

name: Do
summary: Do[expr, n] evaluates expr n times.

name: Dot
summary: a . b . c or Dot[a, b, c]

name: DownValues
summary: DownValues[s] gives a list of down-value rules for s.

name: Dt
summary: Dt[f] gives the total derivative of f.

name: End
summary: End[] restores the context that was active before the matching Begin[]

name: EndPackage
summary: EndPackage[] restores the state saved by BeginPackage and prepends the

name: EulerPhi
summary: EulerPhi[n] gives the Euler totient function phi(n).

name: EvenQ
summary: EvenQ[n] gives True if n is an even integer (Integer or BigInt), False otherwise.

name: Expand
summary: Expand[expr] expands out products and powers in expr.

name: Factor
summary: Factor[poly] factors a polynomial over the integers.

name: FactorInteger
summary: FactorInteger[n] gives a list of the prime factors of the integer n, together with their exponents.

name: Factorial2
summary: Factorial2[n] (also typeset n!!) gives the double factorial of n.

name: First
summary: First[expr] gives the first element of expr.

name: Fit
summary: Fit[data, {f1, ..., fn}, vars] finds a least-squares fit a1 f1 + ... + an fn to data for functions of the variables vars (a symbol or list of symbols).

name: Flat
summary: Flat is an attribute that can be assigned to a symbol f to indicate that all expressions involving nested functions f should be flattened out. This property is accounted for in pattern matching.

name: For
summary: For[start, test, incr, body] executes start, then repeatedly evaluates body and incr until test fails to give True.

name: FromDigits
summary: FromDigits[list] constructs an integer from a list of decimal digits, most-significant first.

name: GeneratedParameters
summary: GeneratedParameters is an option for Solve specifying the

name: HankelMatrix
summary: HankelMatrix[n] gives the n x n Hankel matrix with first row and column the integers 1..n.

name: HilbertMatrix
summary: HilbertMatrix[n] gives the n x n Hilbert matrix with entries 1/(i + j - 1).

name: HoldForm
summary: HoldForm[expr] prints as the expression expr, with expr maintained in an unevaluated form.

name: Identity
summary: Identity[expr] gives expr unchanged (the identity function).

name: IdentityMatrix
summary: IdentityMatrix[n] gives the n x n identity matrix.

name: Im
summary: Im[z] gives the imaginary part of numeric z, and 0 for real or real-valued (Re/Im/Abs/Arg) arguments.

name: Inequality
summary: Inequality[v0, op0, v1, op1, v2, ...] is the canonical form for a chained comparison such as a < b <= c. It returns True if every adjacent pair holds, False if any pair fails, and otherwise the residual chain with decidable pairs dropped.

name: Information
summary: Information[symbol] or ?symbol returns information on symbol.

name: Insert
summary: Insert[expr, elem, n] inserts elem at position n in expr.

name: IntegerDigits
summary: IntegerDigits[n] gives a list of the decimal digits in the integer n.

name: IntegerExponent
summary: IntegerExponent[n, b] gives the highest power of b that divides n.

name: IntegerLength
summary: IntegerLength[n] gives the number of decimal digits in the integer n.

name: IntegerString
summary: IntegerString[n] gives a string consisting of the decimal digits in the integer n.

name: Integrate
summary: Integrate[f, x] gives the indefinite integral of f with respect to x.

name: InverseFunctions
summary: InverseFunctions is an option for Solve that enables the

name: Last
summary: Last[expr] gives the last element of expr.

name: LeafCount
summary: LeafCount[expr] gives the total number of indivisible subexpressions in expr.

name: ListQ
summary: ListQ[expr] gives True if expr is a list (head List), False otherwise.

name: Longest
summary: Longest[p] is a pattern object that matches the longest sequence consistent with the pattern p.

name: MachineNumberQ
summary: MachineNumberQ[expr] gives True if expr is a machine-precision real or complex number, and False otherwise.

name: MantissaExponent
summary: MantissaExponent[x] gives a list {m, e} containing the mantissa and exponent of the real number x, such that x = m * 10^e and 1/10 <= |m| < 1 (or m = 0 when x = 0).

name: Mean
summary: Mean[data] gives the mean estimate of the elements in data.

name: MemberQ
summary: MemberQ[list, form] returns True if an element of list matches form, and False otherwise.

name: Module
summary: Module[{x, y, ...}, expr] specifies that x, y, ... are local variables.

name: Most
summary: Most[expr] gives all but the last element of expr.

name: NextPrime
summary: NextPrime[x] gives the next prime after x.

name: NumericQ
summary: NumericQ[expr] gives True if expr is a numeric quantity, and False otherwise.

name: OddQ
summary: OddQ[n] gives True if n is an odd integer (Integer or BigInt), False otherwise.

name: OneIdentity
summary: OneIdentity is an attribute that can be assigned to a symbol f to indicate that f[x], f[f[x]], etc. are all equivalent to x for the purpose of pattern matching.

name: OrderedQ
summary: OrderedQ[h[e1, e2, ...]] gives True if the elements are in canonical order, and False otherwise.

name: Orderless
summary: Orderless is an attribute that can be assigned to a symbol f to indicate that the elements e_i in expressions of the form f[e_1, e_2, ...] should automatically be sorted into canonical order. This property is accounted for in pattern matching.

name: OwnValues
summary: OwnValues[s] gives a list of own-value rules for s.

name: Plus
summary: x + y + ... or Plus[x, y, ...] represents a sum of terms.

name: PolynomialExtendedGCD
summary: PolynomialExtendedGCD[poly1, poly2, x] gives the extended GCD of poly1 and poly2 treated as univariate polynomials in x.

name: PolynomialGCD
summary: PolynomialGCD[poly1, poly2, ...] gives the greatest common divisor of the polynomials.

name: PolynomialLCM
summary: PolynomialLCM[poly1, poly2, ...] gives the least common multiple of the polynomials.

name: PolynomialQuotient
summary: PolynomialQuotient[p, q, x] gives the quotient of p and q, treated as polynomials in x, with any remainder dropped.

name: PolynomialRemainder
summary: PolynomialRemainder[p, q, x] gives the remainder from dividing p by q, treated as polynomials in x.

name: Position
summary: Position[expr, pattern] gives a list of the positions at which objects matching pattern appear in expr.

name: PossibleZeroQ
summary: PossibleZeroQ[expr] gives True if symbolic and numerical methods suggest that expr has value zero, and False otherwise.

name: Power
summary: x ^ y or Power[x, y] represents x to the power y.

name: PowerMod
summary: PowerMod[a, b, m] gives a^b mod m.

name: Prepend
summary: Prepend[expr, elem] adds elem to the beginning of expr.

name: PrimePi
summary: PrimePi[x] gives the number of primes less than or equal to x.

name: Quartics
summary: Quartics is an option for Solve that controls whether quartic

name: Re
summary: Re[z] gives the real part of numeric z; Re[Re[z]], Re[Im[z]], Re[Abs[z]], Re[Arg[z]] fold since those heads are real-valued.

name: ReIm
summary: ReIm[z] gives {Re[z], Im[z]}, the real and imaginary parts of numeric z as a list; real-valued arguments give {z, 0}.

name: RealDigits
summary: RealDigits[x] gives a list {digits, exp} of the digits in the approximate real number x together with the exponent such that the first digit is the coefficient of 10^(exp - 1).

name: RealExponent
summary: RealExponent[x] gives Log[10, |x|] -- the base-10 real exponent of x.

name: Repeated
summary: p.. or Repeated[p] is a pattern object that represents a sequence of one or more expressions, each matching p.

name: RepeatedNull
summary: p... or RepeatedNull[p] is a pattern object that represents a sequence of zero or more expressions, each matching p.

name: RepeatedTiming
summary: RepeatedTiming[expr] evaluates expr repeatedly and returns a list of the average time in seconds used, together with the result obtained.

name: ReplaceList
summary: ReplaceList[expr, rules] attempts to transform the entire expression expr by applying a rule or list of rules in all possible ways, and returns a list of the results obtained.

name: Rest
summary: Rest[expr] gives all but the first element of expr.

name: Reverse
summary: Reverse[expr] reverses the order of elements in expr.

name: RootMeanSquare
summary: RootMeanSquare[list] gives the root mean square of values in list.

name: RotateLeft
summary: RotateLeft[expr, n] rotates the elements of expr n positions to the left.

name: RotateRight
summary: RotateRight[expr, n] rotates the elements of expr n positions to the right.

name: SeriesData
summary: SeriesData[x, x0, {a0, a1, ...}, nmin, nmax, den]

name: SetAttributes
summary: SetAttributes[s, attr] sets the attributes for s.

name: Shortest
summary: Shortest[p] is a pattern object that matches the shortest sequence consistent with the pattern p.

name: Sign
summary: Sign[x] gives -1, 0, or 1 for real numeric x according to its sign, and z/Abs[z] for a nonzero numeric complex z.

name: Slot
summary: # or Slot[n] represents the n-th argument of a pure function.

name: SlotSequence
summary: ## or SlotSequence[n] represents arguments from the n-th onward.

name: Sort
summary: Sort[list] sorts the elements of list into canonical order.

name: Span
summary: i;;j represents a span of elements i through j. i;;j;;k represents a span in steps of k.

name: StandardDeviation
summary: StandardDeviation[data] gives the standard deviation estimate of the elements in data.

name: SubresultantPolynomialRemainders
summary: SubresultantPolynomialRemainders[a, b, x] gives the polynomial-remainder

name: Subtract
summary: x - y or Subtract[x, y] represents x - y; rewritten by the evaluator

name: Sum
summary: Sum[f, {i, imax}] gives the sum of f for i from 1 to imax. Sum[f, {i, imin, imax}], Sum[f, {i, imin, imax, di}] and Sum[f, {i, {i1, i2, ...}}] use the standard iterator forms; multiple iterators give nested sums. Sum[f, i] gives the indefinite sum (antidifference). Symbolic and infinite sums are evaluated in closed form via Method -> "Polynomial" | "Geometric" | "Gosper".

name: Tally
summary: Tally[list] counts the number of occurrences of each distinct element in list.

name: Times
summary: x * y * ... or Times[x, y, ...] represents a product of terms.

name: Timing
summary: Timing[expr] evaluates expr, and returns a list of the time in seconds used, together with the result obtained.

name: ToeplitzMatrix
summary: ToeplitzMatrix[n] gives the n x n Toeplitz matrix with first row and column the integers 1..n.

name: Together
summary: Together[expr] combines fractions over a common denominator, then cancels.

name: TrueQ
summary: TrueQ[expr] yields True if expr is True, and False otherwise.

name: VandermondeMatrix
summary: VandermondeMatrix[{x1, ..., xn}] gives the n x n Vandermonde matrix with entry (i, j) equal to xi^(j-1).

name: Variance
summary: Variance[data] gives the unbiased variance estimate of the elements in data.

name: VerifySolutions
summary: VerifySolutions is an option for Solve that decides whether to

name: While
summary: While[test, body] evaluates test, then body, repeatedly, until test first fails to give True.

name: With
summary: With[{x = x0, ...}, expr] specifies that x should be replaced by x0 throughout expr.
